title: manifest,test: `inspect` should contain `OCI` annotations.
body:
Verifies https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1105 by adding a new test `inspect manifest and verify OCI annotation`
and old tests should not break.


#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
manifest: inspect now supports OCI annotations
```

comments.body: /hold this is just to verify https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1105 so do not merge plz

> /hold this is just to verify [containers/common#1105](https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1105) so do not merge plz

Needs a test for exercising the new fields

> > /hold this is just to verify [containers/common#1105](https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1105) so do not merge plz
> 
> Needs a test for exercising the new fields

Added a test and https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1105 also contains a test. But this points to my fork so lets wait for `c/common` PR.
Tests seem to fail reproducibly.  I did not have a look at the failures though.

Couple of unrelated `podman signature` failures , is it `c/image` cause I independently tried bumping `c/common` before the last commit and it was all green then. Also curious why is it only failing on `ubuntu`.
Bumped to `v0.49.1` instead of a commit in between lets see if CI is happy now or not.

I think this should be green now since `c/common` commit which i need is already here and PR https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15108 also includes that commit which is already green so if https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15108 gets merged before this, I'll drop vendor changes from here and will only add test.

I think https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15108 is needed before I can bump vendors here, @mtrmac Could you correct me if i am assuming this wrong since I see unrelated `signature` tests failing.

Edit: please ignore this message.
Needed feature is already in here: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15108 so dropping vendor changes and just adding test.

Is podman upstream broken ? Current PR does not adds anything other than a new test and some unrelated tests old are failing.

All failing tests have common
```
Error: error storing signature for docker.io/library/alpine:latest, sha256:fa93b01658e3a5a1686dc3ae55f170d8de487006fb53a28efcd12ab0710a2e5f: Unusable secret key
```
Yes I see same failures in other PR's https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15136
I don't really understand why.  Did the key expire? Cc: @Luap99

```
$ gpg --list-secret-keys            
[...] sec   rsa2048 2020-07-31 [SC] [expired: 2022-07-31] 
```

Yes, the key expired yesterday.

> #15139 will fix it

@vrothberg thanks :) I'll rebase once #15139 gets merged.
This should become green now ( hopefully ).
